request.prompt:
REVIEW-ONLY: validate the implementation spec for build task 001 of feature robo-chat-model. Do NOT modify any code or files. Reply 'AGREE' if the spec is sound and implementable, otherwise list concrete objections each tagged critical / major / suggestion (only critical/major block; critical/major MUST cite file:line + a concrete failure scenario, else it is a suggestion). Do NOT flag style/preference. 

TASK 001 SPEC:
- Set package.json engines.vscode to ^1.122.0.
- Bump @types/vscode devDependency to ^1.122.0 and update package-lock.json so the project type-checks against the new API surface.
- Add the contributes.languageModelChatProviders contribution point (vendor id + displayName for RoboRewsty). NO provider registration code in this task (that is task 006); manifest contribution + version floor only.
- Do NOT touch participant/tools/runtime code.
Allowed files: package.json, package-lock.json, optional colocated *.test.ts for an engines-floor assertion.
Done-check: (1) node assertion that engines.vscode and @types/vscode both match ^1.122 and contributes.languageModelChatProviders exists; (2) npm run compile exits 0; (3) npm run test:unit exits 0.

CONTEXT (locked decisions, do not re-litigate): The LanguageModelChatProvider API requires the ^1.122.0 floor so BYOK/extension-contributed models work signed-out (corroborated via VS Code 1.122 release + GitHub changelog). The contribution-point key is 'languageModelChatProviders'. Validate only: is this task self-contained, correctly scoped, and is the done-check sufficient to prove it? Focus objections on real implementability blockers (e.g. wrong manifest key name, missing required manifest field that would fail activation/compile).
